behavior_summary: U8P_KITH::look is a short U8P_KITH::look/examine handler for the NPC: when the player inspects the NPC it emits a single bark text "kith" and then the script yields/suspends.
There are no menu choices, flag reads or writes, parameters, or local state used by this handler — it is single-purpose and non-branching.
notable_control_flow: U8P_KITH::look executes on the engine's U8P_KITH::look/examine event, issues one bark line ('kith'), then calls suspend to pause or end the process.
No conditional branches, loops, or external decision points are present; execution is linear: bark -> suspend.
open_questions: Does suspend return control later or terminate the process in this engine context (suspend appears to pause/yield but exact lifecycle is not present in the script)?
Is the bark audio/visual presentation blocking until complete, or does suspend ensure the process stays resident until the bark finishes?
Are there any engine-side implicit flags or state changes triggered by the U8P_KITH::look process that are not visible in the script?

main_function.name: U8P_KITH::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short response when the player examines/looks at this NPC: play the bark 'kith' and then yield/suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine U8P_KITH::look/examine event for the NPC (process type 0x0208).
Runs when the player inspects or 'looks' at the NPC.
